_meta.unit: Early Task 4 (big-five sweep, house 5 of 5) - Gucci lexicon: models (incl. collabs) + materials/signature-motifs + named seasonal signatures/colors

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). Gucci leans on dated heritage names (Bamboo 1947, Horsebit 1955, Jackie 1961)
types.material: signature motif / canvas / leather / hardware - the qualifier vocabulary (GG Supreme, Web stripe, Horsebit, Bamboo handle, Diamante/Guccissima)
types.color: named seasonal signature or genuinely-named house color (Gucci does NOT name most per-season colors; Rosso Ancora is the exception)

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. Model and motif kept as separate facts (Horsebit motif 1953 loafer vs Horsebit bag 1955; GG monogram patent 1969 vs Jackie bag 1961). Honest finding: like Dior, Gucci does not publish a unique official color name per season for most bags; GG Marmont/Dionysus/Jackie rotate plain descriptors. The one genuine named house color is Rosso Ancora (Sabato De Sarno, SS2024 debut). Named layer is small + motif/collab/house-color-led, not invented. Christie's has NO dedicated Gucci artist/handbag-history page (the /artists/gucci/gucci-handbag URL redirects to generic search); the Gucci 'stories' collecting-guide page IS the auction-grade source. gucci.com NOT scraped (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior runs); season pages queued for owner-present Chrome path. Live cultural fact: 3 creative directors in ~3 years - De Sarno (2023, SS24 debut), then Demna (2025-, digital lookbook + film 'The Tiger').
sources: facebook.com/GUCCI (Sylvie SS2016 post; Blondie Interlocking-G 1971 post; Design Ancora in Rosso Ancora post)

coverage.colors: 4 named signatures (Rosso Ancora + Flora + Ken Scott + Hacker finishes) - Gucci does not name most per-season colors

name: Bamboo 1947 / Bamboo
note: Original heat-bent bamboo-handle top-handle bag; born 1947 (orig. product no. '0633') from a post-war leather shortage. Reissued Feb 2022 as 'Bamboo 1947'. Frida Giannini's 'New Bamboo' came SS2010 (luisaviaroma). Bamboo handle = a separate motif (see materials).

name: Horsebit 1955
note: Small flap shoulder bag carrying the horsebit (double-ring-and-bar) clasp; the bag dates to 1955 (motif first on a 1953 loafer). Modern 'Horsebit 1955' line relaunched under Michele (~2021). Tom Ford-era Horsebits (early 2000s) are Y2K-resurgence collector pieces.

name: Jackie 1961 / Jackie (vintage)
note: Crescent-shaped hobo with a cylindrical piston closure; first introduced 1961, orig. 'Fifties Constance', renamed for Jackie Kennedy after a paparazzi photo. Reissued with more structure as 'Jackie 1961' by Michele 2020-21; softer/slouchier 'Jackie' by Demna 2025. Christie's confirms 1961.

name: Diana
note: Bamboo-handle tote with removable colored leather belts on the handles; debuted 1991 (beige suede), a Princess Diana favorite. Reissued by Michele 2021 in three sizes. Marie Claire also dates it 1991. Uses the bamboo handle motif (see materials).

name: Dionysus
note: Structured shoulder/chain bag with the tiger-head horseshoe (spur) clasp; named for the Greek god Dionysus. Alessandro Michele's first It-bag, 2015 (SS2016). Successor in spirit to Tom Ford's 90s dragon-head chain bag. Vogue credits Michele.

name: GG Marmont
note: Matelassé chevron-quilted flap with the antique-gold Double-G logo (from 70s Gucci belts); named for the Chateau Marmont hotel. Debuted on the catwalk 2016 under Alessandro Michele. Sotheby's lists it among the must-have Gucci bags.

name: Ophidia
note: GG Supreme canvas line with the green-red-green Web stripe and a Tom Ford-era pin closure; boxy totes, mini shoulder, small Boston. Introduced Cruise 2018 under Alessandro Michele; has grown year on year. Vogue confirms Cruise 2018.

name: Sylvie / Sylvie 1969
note: Top-handle/shoulder bag with a Web stripe and a chain-and-buckle closure; 'Sylvie 1969' is the smaller vintage-shape (60s/70s archive) version. Debuted SS2016 by Michele, first seen Sept 2015 Milan show (official Gucci post). Sotheby's lists Sylvie 1969.

name: Padlock
note: GG Supreme/leather shoulder bag with a horsebit-detail padlock closure. Introduced as part of the SS2016 collection (Michele); Sotheby's ranks it among the top Gucci bags by resale value.

name: Soho / Soho Disco
note: Round/boxy crossbody (Disco) and tote (Soho) with an embossed interlocking-G medallion; the cult 'reasonably priced luxury crossbody.' Released 2012 by Frida Giannini. Distinct from but a forerunner of the Blondie.

name: Blondie
note: Round Interlocking-G-medallion bag line; the Interlocking G emblem was originally patented 1971. Relaunched as the Blondie line by Alessandro Michele 2022 (official Gucci post).

name: Attache
note: Slim elongated half-moon shoulder bag with a Web stripe and a vintage-G pull; a Michele archive revival. Vogue groups it with the Diana and Jackie as Michele reissues (~2021). Marie Claire lists the Attache.

name: Zumi
note: Rounded top-handle/shoulder bag with a horsebit-and-bar closure; named for a Michele muse. ~2019 debut is established beat, not firmly resourced this run.

name: Bree
note: GG Supreme canvas bag with leather trim and Web; a Giannini-era line (~2013). Established beat, debut year not firmly resourced this run.

name: Aphrodite
note: Slouchy hobo/shoulder bag with crystal-Double-G hardware; a Michele late-era release (~2022). Established beat, debut year not firmly resourced this run.

name: Queen Margaret
note: GG Supreme/leather top-handle with a metal bee and a Web; a Michele archive-flora release (~2018-2019). Cited in brand-naming-research.md (Yoogi's). Established beat, exact year not firmly resourced.

name: Boston
note: Classic barrel-shaped GG/Web doctor-bag silhouette that recurs across eras (vintage GG Boston, modern Ophidia small Boston). A silhouette name, not a single dated launch.

name: GG emblem / GG Supreme totes
maps_to: GG Supreme tote / GG emblem tote / bucket
note: Open totes and bucket bags in GG Supreme coated canvas with the Web; the everyday workhorse format across eras, incl. the current De Sarno-era 'GG emblem' line (tote/bucket per Vogue product cards). A format, not a single dated launch.

name: GG Milano
note: Polished structured top-handle introduced by Sabato De Sarno (~2023-2024), reflecting his streamlined vision. Vogue credits De Sarno.

name: Madison / Borsetto / Mercato
note: New styles introduced by Demna for Gucci: the Madison shoulder bag, the Borsetto, and the Mercato tote, around his Cruise 2027 era (2025-2026). Vogue attributes them to Demna.

name: The Hacker Project (Gucci x Balenciaga)
maps_to: Balenciaga Hourglass / Gucci Jackie 1961 / Hacker pouch
note: Gucci 'hacking' Balenciaga and vice versa across base models: Balenciaga Hourglass in Gucci GG/Flora, Gucci Jackie 1961 in Balenciaga BB monogram, plus the Hacker pouch. Part of the Aria collection for Gucci's 100th anniversary; unveiled Apr 2021, retail Nov 2021. Sotheby's dates a Hacker Hour Glass bag to 2021.

name: Gucci x adidas
note: adidas Trefoil + Gucci GG/Web co-branding across Horsebit 1955, Ophidia, and duffle/holdall styles. Exquisite Gucci (FW22, Michele). Established beat; exact season queued for the collab press release.

name: Gucci x Disney
maps_to: GG Supreme tote / Ophidia / mini bag
note: Mickey Mouse (2020 Chinese New Year, 'Year of the Rat') and Donald Duck (2024) capsules on GG Supreme totes, Ophidia, mini bags. Established beat; exact drops queued for confirmation.

name: Gucci x The North Face
note: Floral/GG print outdoor capsule across totes, backpacks, duffles. First drop 2021, second 2022. Established beat; exact drops queued for confirmation.

name: Diamante
note: The original 1930s repeated geometric diamond pattern (dark brown on tan canvas); the ancestor of the GG monogram. Christie's: the GG monogram is an update of Gucci's 1930s Diamante design.

name: GG monogram canvas (vintage)
note: The interlocking reversed double-G trellis jacquard (sandy taupe), an update of the 1930s Diamante; the patent was filed 1969. The 'GG canvas' of the 60s-90s. Christie's confirms the 1969 patent.

name: GG Supreme canvas
note: The modern coated-canvas version of the GG monogram (more durable, beige/ebony); the base for Ophidia, Dionysus, Padlock, GG totes. The current everyday Gucci canvas.

name: Diamante / Guccissima
note: The embossed/tonal version of the Diamante diamond pressed into leather as 'Guccissima'; a Frida Giannini-era signature leather (~2006). On Soho, totes, wallets. Shares the same diamond DNA as the GG monogram. Year is established beat.

name: Web stripe (green-red-green)
note: The signature ribbon stripe, first seen early 1950s; a reinterpretation of the equestrian girth strap (the band holding a horse's saddle). Across Ophidia, Sylvie, Attache, vintage Jackie; sometimes a blue-red-blue variant. Christie's dates it early 1950s; year ~1951 approximate.

name: Horsebit hardware
note: The double-ring-and-bar metal clasp from English equestrian bridles; first appeared on a Gucci loafer in 1953, then the Horsebit bag in 1955. Christie's confirms the 1953 loafer.

name: Bamboo handle
note: The heat-bent burnished bamboo cane handle, born of a 1947 post-war leather shortage; still hand-bent over flame by Gucci artisans, and patented by Gucci. Christie's confirms 1947 + the technique. Distinct from the bags (Bamboo 1947, Diana) that use it.

name: Matelassé chevron (GG Marmont quilting)
note: The soft chevron/herringbone matelassé quilting on the GG Marmont, with the antique-gold Double-G logo. Debuted with the GG Marmont 2016.

name: Flora
note: The botanical floral print, created as a silk scarf for Grace Kelly in the 1960s (~1966); 'remains a house code to this day' per Vogue. Rotates seasonally across bags and scarves.

name: Microguccissima
note: The fine, small-scale embossed Guccissima (mini diamond) on leather SLGs and small bags. Giannini/Michele-era. Established beat; not auction-confirmed this run.

name: Rosso Ancora (as a house finish/code)
status: house color used as a finish code
note: The De Sarno-era house red (deep oxblood); not strictly a material but used as a house finish/code across leathers and jacquards. See the color row for the full naming detail. Logged in both layers because it functions as a surface signature.

name: Rosso Ancora ("Gucci Rosso")
note: Sabato De Sarno's signature deep oxblood red, debuted at his SS2024 show (Sept 2023), named to mark his creative chapter ('ancora' = 'again', also 'still/more'). Used across leathers, jacquards, and the 'Gucci Ancora'/'Design Ancora' campaigns. A color 'Gucci has used before,' pulled from the archive. Vogue + official Gucci posts + Hero Magazine confirm. THE one genuinely-named Gucci house color.

name: Flora (print, seasonal rotations)
note: The botanical Flora print rotates seasonally across bags/scarves; a house code (1960s origin), not a per-season color name. Logged as a named print signature, not a colorway.

name: Ken Scott (GG Marmont print)
note: GG Marmont in vivid Ken Scott archival floral prints, a named licensed-artist print (Epilogue/Aria era, 2021), not a per-season color name.

name: The Hacker Project (Balenciaga "hacked" finishes)
note: GG/Flora/BB-monogram 'hacked' surfaces on base models (Hourglass, Jackie); the named capsule IS the finish identity. 2021. Sotheby's catalogues a Multicolor Blooms Canvas Hacker Hour Glass bag, 2021.
